is a [civil township](/wiki/Civil_township "Civil township") of [Gogebic County](/wiki/Gogebic_County,_Michigan "Gogebic County, Michigan") in the [U.S. state](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") of [Michigan](/wiki/Michigan "Michigan"). The population was 455 at the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census"), a significant decrease from 1,727 at the [2010 census](/wiki/2010_United_States_census "2010 United States census").

**Marenisco Township** is a [civil township](/wiki/Civil_township "Civil township") of [Gogebic County](/wiki/Gogebic_County,_Michigan "Gogebic County, Michigan") in the [U.S. state](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") of [Michigan](/wiki/Michigan "Michigan"). The population was 455 at the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census"), a significant decrease from 1,727 at the [2010 census](/wiki/2010_United_States_census "2010 United States census").

The [unincorporated community](/wiki/Unincorporated_area#United_States "Unincorporated area") of [Marenisco](/wiki/Marenisco,_Michigan "Marenisco, Michigan") is within the township on the [Presque Isle River](/wiki/Presque_Isle_River "Presque Isle River") near the junction of [U.S. Highway 2](/wiki/U.S._Route_2_in_Michigan "U.S. Route 2 in Michigan") and [M-64](/wiki/M-64_(Michigan_highway) "M-64 (Michigan highway)") at [46°22′35″N 89°41′47″W﻿ / ﻿46.37639°N 89.69639°W﻿ / 46.37639; -89.69639](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Marenisco_Township,_Michigan&params=46_22_35_N_89_41_47_W_).[[3]](#cite_note-3) [Wakefield](/wiki/Wakefield,_Michigan "Wakefield, Michigan") is about 15 miles (24 km) northwest, and [Watersmeet](/wiki/Watersmeet,_Michigan "Watersmeet, Michigan") about 27 miles (43 km) southeast on US 2.

The name was formed by combining the first three letters of the first, middle, and last names of Mary Enid Scott, the wife of E. H. Scott, a timber producer.[[4]](#cite_note-Romig-4)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Marenisco_Township,_Michigan&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Geography")]

According to the [United States Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau"), the township has a total area of 325.9 square miles (844 km2), of which 310.9 square miles (805 km2) is land and 15.0 square miles (38.9 km2 or 4.61%) is water. It is the second-largest township in land area in Michigan (after [McMillan Township](/wiki/McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan "McMillan Township, Luce County, Michigan") in [Luce County](/wiki/Luce_County,_Michigan "Luce County, Michigan")).

### Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Marenisco_Township,_Michigan&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Climate")]

The climate is described as [Humid Continental](/wiki/Humid_continental_climate "Humid continental climate") by the [Köppen Climate System](/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_climate_classification "Köppen climate classification"), abbreviated as *Dfb*.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Marenisco_Township,_Michigan&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Demographics")]

As of the [census](/wiki/Census "Census")[[1]](#cite_note-GR2-1) of 2000, there were 1,051 people, 264 households, and 186 families residing in the township. In 2020, there were 455 people in the township.

## Transportation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Marenisco_Township,_Michigan&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Transportation")]

### Major highways

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Marenisco_Township,_Michigan&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Major highways")]

* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/27/US_2.svg/40px-US_2.svg.png) [US 2](/wiki/U.S._Route_2_in_Michigan "U.S. Route 2 in Michigan")
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a5/M-64.svg/40px-M-64.svg.png) [M-64](/wiki/M-64_(Michigan_highway) "M-64 (Michigan highway)")

### Bus service

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Marenisco_Township,_Michigan&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Bus service")]

* [Indian Trails](/wiki/Indian_Trails "Indian Trails") provides daily intercity bus service between [St. Ignace](/wiki/St._Ignace,_Michigan "St. Ignace, Michigan") and [Ironwood, Michigan](/wiki/Ironwood,_Michigan "Ironwood, Michigan").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Notable people

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Marenisco_Township,_Michigan&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Notable people")]

* [Arthur E. Stadler](/wiki/Arthur_E._Stadler "Arthur E. Stadler"), (1892-?) was a member of the Wisconsin State Assembly, chairman of the town board of Owen, chairman and supervisor of the Clark County, Wisconsin Board[[7]](#cite_note-7)
